folio.label: 140r
folio.l: Bothe of hir beaute , and hire bounte wyde
That thurgú that lond , they preised hirö echone
That loued vertu , saue Envye allone
That sory is , of oother mennes wele
And glad is of his sorwe , and his vnhele
The doctoå , makitú this discripcioun
This mayde vp on a day , went in the toun
Toward a temple , witú hirö moder deere
As is of ©onge maydens , the manere
¢ Now was there thanne , a Iustice in that toun
That gouernour was , of that Regioun
And so bifel , this Iuge , his eyen caste
Vp on this mayde , auysynge hi~ ful faste
As she coom forby , there as this Iuge stod
A noon his herte chaunged , and his mood
So was he caugút , witú beaute of this mayde
And to hi~ self , ful pryuely he sayde
This mayde shal be myn , for any man
A noon the fend , in to his herte ran
And taugúte hiö sodeynly , that he by sleigúte
The mayden to his purpos , wynne mygúte
For certes by no force , ne by no mede
Hi~ thougúte he was nat able , for to spede
For she was strongö of frendes , and eke she
Confermed was , in swicú souäayn bounte
That wel he wiste , he mygút hir neuäe wynne
As for to make hirö , witú hir body synne
For whicú , by greet deliberacioun
He sent after a cherl , was in the toun
Whicú that he knew , for subtil and for bold
This Iuge vn to this cherl , his tale hatú told
In secree wyse , and maad hi~ to ensure
He shulde telle it , to no creature
And if he dide , he shulde leese his hed
Whan that assented was , this cursed reed
Glad was this Iuge , and maked hi~ gret chere
And ©af hi~ ©iftes , päciouse and deere
¢ Whan shapen was , al here conspiracye
Fro poynt to poynt , how that his lecherye
Parfourmed shulde ben , ful subtilly
As ©e shuln heere it , after openly
¢ Hom gotú the cherl , that higút Claudyus
This fals Iuge , that higút Apius
So was his name , for this is no fable
